title: MediConnect
summary: A role-based hospital management system built to practice scalable backend design and access control.
problem: Managing different user roles (patients, admins) with secure data access and clean separation of responsibilities is complex in full-stack systems.
solution: Developed a MERN application implementing JWT authentication, role-based authorization, protected routes, and modular backend architecture to simulate real-world access control scenarios.
features: Patient and admin dashboards with role-specific capabilities
Secure authorization gates for sensitive routes
Structured backend schema for maintainable data ownership
Workflow-centric information architecture for faster operations

title: JobQuest
summary: A full-stack job portal built to simulate real-world hiring workflows and notification systems.
problem: Designing consistent workflows for job posting, applications, and user roles while maintaining data integrity across the system.
solution: Built a MERN application with role-based authentication, structured job/application schemas, and automated email notifications using Node Cron to handle asynchronous updates.
features: Role-based access model for seeker and employer journeys
Validated job posting and application pipelines
Scheduled alert notifications for timely opportunities
Responsive UX across core job discovery and application flows

title: QTrip Dynamic
summary: An interactive travel exploration web app focused on frontend performance and dynamic UI behavior.
problem: Building responsive and interactive filtering systems while maintaining smooth UI updates and state consistency.
solution: Implemented dynamic filtering, conditional rendering, and API-based reservation handling using vanilla JavaScript and Fetch API, focusing on efficient DOM updates and user experience.
features: Multi-select filter system for tailored adventure discovery
Interactive image carousel and dynamic UI updates
Reservation request integration via Fetch API
